deck: Tim Wood, the Senior Program Officer who led the Gates Foundation's investment in Dimagi, joins Jonathan Jackson to tell the story of a rare platform-level grant that funded CommCare itself rather than another single project. Part two of Dimagi's 20th anniversary series.
seo_description: Tim Wood and Jonathan Jackson on how the Gates Foundation's core funding of CommCare became Dimagi's big break, and what it takes to fund digital public goods.
og_description: How the Gates Foundation's core funding of CommCare became Dimagi's big break, with former Senior Program Officer Tim Wood.

pull_quote.text: It was really focused on how does the CommCare platform get stronger and bigger and more sustainable. And that was a new type of grant for the gates foundation to be making.
pull_quote.cite: Tim Wood, on the 2016 platform grant

bullets: From Microsoft to global health: how a five-week trek through Nepal and an early part-time role led Tim Wood to leave product development behind
Breaking the project mold: why a grant funding the CommCare platform itself, not a single malaria or maternal health app, was so unusual for the Gates Foundation
Pilotitis explained: the syndrome of money pumped into endless new pilots instead of investing in scale
The scale-revenue disconnect: why more users on national-scale projects often meant more cost and liability, not more profit
Making the internal case: how a cross-cutting integrated delivery team let Tim pitch a tool that could lift impact across multiple health domains
Advice for social entrepreneurs: aligning with a funder's evolving priorities, building local capacity, and being honest about what you can and cannot solve
The paper-to-digital problem: why a real plan to go paperless is critical, and the tax that pilots place on overworked frontline workers

stats.label: core funding the Gates Foundation invested in Dimagi across 2014 and 2016
users on the CASS project in India that Tim cited as proof of CommCare's scale
of Dimagi history this anniversary series looks back on

guests.name: Tim Wood
guests.role: Former Senior Program Officer at the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation who led the platform-level investment in Dimagi; now a senior advisor working on collaborative funding for digital public goods.
